title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan Dies At 100
stories.summary:
Alan Greenspan, the influential economist and former chairman of the Federal Reserve, has died at the age of 100. His wife, NBC News anchor Andrea Mitchell, confirmed that he passed away at home from complications related to Parkinson’s disease.

Greenspan served as Fed chair for five terms between 1987 and 2006, a period during which he significantly expanded the central bank's power and reshaped monetary policy. Described by outlets like Al Jazeera and the BBC as one of the most influential economic policymakers in modern U.S. history, his tenure left a lasting mark on the American economy.

While some coverage highlights his role in building the modern Federal Reserve, others focus on his broader legacy as a giant who helped shape economic frameworks for decades. His death marks the end of an era for central banking and macroeconomic policy.

stories.title: Another Trump-Backed Candidate Declares Victory Over The Left In Latin America
stories.summary:
Colombia has elected right-wing lawyer Abelardo De la Espriella as its next president, marking a significant shift away from the four-year rule of a far-left administration. Initial results indicate that De la Espriella narrowly defeated leftist Senator Iván Cepeda in Sunday’s presidential runoff election.

The Trump administration quickly congratulated De la Espriella following his victory, which he secured with approximately 49.7% of the vote. Conservative outlets have characterized the win as a triumph for right-leaning candidates backed by former President Donald Trump across Latin America.

Despite the preliminary results pointing to a clear outcome, Cepeda has stated that the current count is not yet official or binding. This discrepancy highlights ongoing tensions regarding the finalization of election results in the South American nation.

links.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
links.excerpt: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.title: Ransom note about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance says she died, CNN reports
stories.summary:
A ransom note regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, the 84-year-old mother of "Today" show host Savannah Guthrie, claims she has died, according to CNN and other news organizations citing law enforcement sources. The BBC reports that the message from the suspected kidnappers included an apology to the family and stated they did not intend for her death.

However, conflicting accounts have emerged regarding the specific contents of the note. Harvey Levin told Fox News that reports claiming the ransom letter contained an apology or a statement about her burial were inaccurate based on the version received by TMZ.

links.headline: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
links.excerpt: The note from the possible kidnappers reportedly stated that they did not mean for her to die and included an apology to the family.

stories.title: Multiple People Killed After Gunman Opens Fire In Jewish District
stories.summary:
A gunman opened fire Monday in Montreal's Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood, a heavily Jewish district, resulting in multiple fatalities and injuries. The incident occurred shortly before noon when police responded to reports of an armed individual near a local intersection.

Authorities confirmed that one police officer and one civilian were killed during the shooting. A second police officer was critically injured and remains fighting for his life in the hospital.

The suspected gunman was also killed by law enforcement at the scene. The attack has drawn attention from multiple news outlets, including Al Jazeera, Breitbart, and Daily Wire, all reporting on the tragic event.

links.headline: Video shows deadly shooting in Montreal, Canada
links.excerpt: A police officer, a civilian and the suspected gunman were killed after a shooting in Montreal.

stories.title: Top auto regulator opens special probe after a Tesla slams into a Texas home, killing a 76-year-old
stories.summary:
The top U.S. auto regulator has opened a special investigation following a fatal crash involving a Tesla in Texas. The incident occurred when the vehicle, reportedly operating with an automated driving feature enabled, struck a home at high speed.

A 76-year-old woman standing inside the residence was killed in the collision. Authorities are examining whether the self-driving technology contributed to the accident and the subsequent failure to avoid the structure.

links.excerpt: Road safety regulator investigates crash involving a Model 3 that had been reportedly operating in self-driving mode.

stories.title: Teenage girl on the way to a high school in Gaza killed in Israeli strike, relatives say
stories.summary:
A 17-year-old Palestinian girl was killed in an Israeli strike on Monday while walking to her high school in Gaza City, relatives and media reports confirmed. Raghad Ashour was en route to sit for a scheduled exam when the incident occurred.

Al Jazeera reported that the teenager was targeted as she made her way to the educational facility. The outlet identified the victim by name and noted the circumstances surrounding the strike.

Relatives speaking to the Washington Times corroborated the account, stating that the girl was killed while walking to take an exam at her high school. Both sources attribute the fatality directly to Israeli military action in the area.

stories.title: Iran war live: Ghalibaf says US to release $12bn in frozen Iranian funds
stories.summary:
Iranian officials stated that the United States is set to release $12 billion in frozen Iranian funds, a move linked to Tehran's alleged commitment to permit international nuclear inspections. This announcement emerged from diplomatic discussions aimed at de-escalating tensions between the two nations.

However, conflicting accounts have surfaced regarding the specifics of these negotiations. The BBC reported that while the US has eased certain oil sanctions, Iran’s foreign ministry explicitly denied making any new commitments concerning nuclear inspections following talks held in Switzerland.

links.excerpt: US says the decisions follow Iran's commitment to allow international nuclear inspections.

stories.title: Consumer-first energy future?
stories.summary:
Australia’s energy landscape is facing renewed scrutiny as major regulatory bodies and economic advisors converge on the need for a consumer-centric approach. The nation's three primary energy regulatory agencies have recently released comprehensive reports outlining recommendations aimed at restructuring market dynamics to prioritize household and small business interests.

Complementing these regulatory findings, the Productivity Commission—the government’s independent economic adviser—has also issued its own set of proposals. This alignment suggests a growing consensus among key institutional stakeholders regarding the necessity of shifting focus toward consumer welfare in energy policy formulation.

stories.title: Pauline Hanson’s media attacks are not just Trump inspired. For 30 years she has sought to control the press | Malcolm Farr
stories.summary:
One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has clarified that she will not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if her party were to come to power. This statement follows a recent address at the National Press Club where she initially opened the door to rolling back the policy.

The clarification comes amidst heightened scrutiny of Hanson’s relationship with the media, particularly after a contentious appearance at the same event. During that appearance, Hanson expressed anger toward reporters who challenged her narrative and specifically vowed never to speak with Guardian Australia again after an inquiry regarding her daughter's employment.

stories.title: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians | Shadi Khan Saif
stories.summary:
A few days before Australia’s population surpassed 28 million, a neighbor and cricket mate invited the author to attend his citizenship ceremony. The event marked the culmination of more than four years spent navigating forms, waiting periods, interviews, and occasional setbacks.

The experience highlighted the quiet pride visible on the faces of new Australians as they formalized their belonging. Rather than creating a sense of connection, the ceremony recognized years of steadily building lives within the community.

links.headline: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians
links.excerpt: Belonging didn’t start that day. The event recognised what had already been lived, the years of steadily building lives A few days before Australia’s population ticked over 28 million, a neighbour and regular cricket mate invited me along to his citizenship ceremony. We arrived early, a little unsure of what the morning would hold. For him, it had been a long road. More than four years of forms, waiting, interviews, setbacks and that quiet, stubborn kind of hope you don’t always t
links.imageAlt: At my friend’s citizenship ceremony, I was moved by the quiet pride in the faces of new Australians | Shadi Khan Saif

stories.title: Streeting’s surrender is bad for Britain
stories.summary:
The Spectator Australia has published an analysis suggesting that recent fiscal decisions by the UK government, particularly regarding Chancellor Rachel Reeves, have negatively impacted British economic stability. The publication highlights a sharp decline in the value of government bonds following the May Budget, indicating significant market unease.

In a separate piece, the outlet challenges common perceptions surrounding voters for Pauline Hanson’s One Nation party. The article argues that these constituents do not fit the typical stereotypes often attributed to them, presenting a more nuanced view of their demographics and motivations.

stories.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
stories.summary:
SpaceX has entered into a significant compute agreement with Reflection AI, an open source artificial intelligence laboratory. The deal involves substantial financial commitments and access to advanced hardware infrastructure.

Under the terms of the arrangement, Reflection AI will pay $150 million monthly starting July 1, 2026, continuing through 2029. This payment secures immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware.

The resources are located across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center facility situated near Memphis, Tennessee.

links.headline: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab

stories.title: Here’s how you can reserve a Steam Machine
stories.summary:
Google DeepMind has entered into a partnership with film studio A24 to develop artificial intelligence tools for movie production. The collaboration aims to integrate advanced AI capabilities directly into the filmmaking workflow, potentially streamlining various stages of content creation.

The deal is reported to be non-exclusive, allowing both parties to pursue other partnerships while working together on multiple projects. This strategic move highlights the growing intersection between major technology firms and traditional entertainment studios as they explore new creative applications for generative AI.

stories.title: New York City House primary emerges as key battleground in ‘AI civil war’
stories.summary:
The artificial intelligence industry is directing significant financial resources toward the 2026 midterm elections, aiming to shape the initial legislative framework governing its sector. AI-focused Super Pacs have collectively raised more than $100 million for this election cycle, with approximately $49 million already deployed across dozens of congressional races nationwide.

New York City’s House primary has emerged as a critical focal point in what some observers are calling an "AI civil war." A substantial portion of these campaign expenditures, nearly half of the total spent so far, is concentrated in a single Manhattan congressional district. This intense financial concentration highlights the industry's strategic priority to secure influence over key lawmakers early in the regulatory process.

stories.title: UK sets out AI infrastructure push at London Tech Week – how does it stack up?
stories.summary:
The UK government has outlined a significant push for artificial intelligence infrastructure during London Tech Week, announcing plans to invest billions into the sector. The initiative aims to bolster domestic capabilities in critical areas such as semiconductor manufacturing and broader tech development.

This move comes amid global competition, with nations seeking to assert greater control over an industry currently dominated by the United States and China. The government hopes to present a compelling growth narrative for the UK's technology landscape.

However, questions remain regarding the practical implementation of these proposals. Critics and observers are scrutinizing how specific plans concerning chip production and social media regulations will effectively translate into tangible economic benefits.

London Tech Week, the showcase event for the UK tech industry, focused heavily on that theme this week. A government keen to show it has a growth story, and an ass

stories.title: Cassette tapes were the voice notes of my youth, bringing tales from the diaspora to our living room | Shadi Khan Saif
stories.summary:
Shadi Khan Saif reflects on the cultural significance of cassette tapes in a personal essay published by The Guardian. Visiting an exhibition at Melbourne's State Library of Victoria, she encountered a display of historical correspondence and was particularly drawn to a collection of vintage audio cassettes.

These recordings served as vital communication tools for diaspora families, allowing distant relatives to share stories and maintain connections across vast distances. Saif describes how these tapes functioned much like modern voice notes, bringing narratives from abroad into the family living room.

The exhibition prompted her to recall childhood memories of gathering with family members to listen to these audio messages. The physical format represented a lost universe of personal history that has largely been replaced by digital communication methods.

links.headline: Cassette tapes were the voice notes of my youth, bringing tales from the diaspora to our living room
links.excerpt: A Melbourne exhibition summons memories of my family gathering to listen to stories from distant relatives On a sunny Melbourne winter morning, I visited the State Library of Victoria to catch up with a mate and stumbled upon an exhibition of heartfelt letters and souvenirs that lovers have been sharing with each throughout the ages. What really caught my eye was a glass box in a neglected corner containing cassette tapes from the past century. It looked like a lost universe redis
links.imageAlt: Cassette tapes were the voice notes of my youth, bringing tales from the diaspora to our living room | Shadi Khan Saif

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Classes suspended in all Tacloban public schools after deadly shooting
stories.summary:
The Department of Education in Tacloban City has suspended classes at all public elementary and secondary schools following a deadly shooting incident. The suspension was announced on Monday night for Tuesday, June 23, after three students were killed and thirteen others wounded at San Jose National High School.

In separate developments regarding educational infrastructure in Mindanao, Rappler reports that an elementary school in Barangay San Jose, General Santos City, remains unusable. The damage occurred after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the region on June 8, leaving parts of the campus barely recognizable.

stories.title: Groups rally for good governance in Cotabato
stories.summary:
Thousands of participants gathered in Cotabato City, BARMM, on Monday for a peaceful rally advocating for good governance and institutional reforms. The demonstration was organized by the Movement to Support Moral Governance and Fight Against Corruption.

Abu Hamza, spokesperson for the organizing group, stated that the gathering aimed to demand stronger accountability and continued investigations into alleged corruption within the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao. The event highlighted public calls for transparency and ethical leadership in regional governance.

links.headline: Groups rally for good governance in Cotabato
links.excerpt: COTABATO CITY, BARMM, Philippines — Thousands joined a peaceful rally here on Monday, demanding good governance, stronger accountability, institutional reforms and continued investigations into alleged corruption within the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). stories.title: DepEd places schools on alert due to Super typhoon Francisco
stories.summary:
The Department of Education has directed schools and field offices nationwide to intensify preparedness measures as Super Typhoon Francisco, known internationally as Mekkhala, remains within the Philippine Area of Responsibility. This directive follows a 5 a.m. bulletin from the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration confirming the storm's continued presence.

At least 95 villages in the provinces of Batanes and Cagayan are currently at risk of flooding and landslides as the typhoon approaches Northern Luzon. In response to these threats, government agencies have ramped up their disaster preparedness efforts.

The Mines and Geosciences Bureau specifically identified 29 barangays in Batanes towns that require immediate attention.

links.headline: 95 villages in Cagayan, Batanes at risk due to Francisco

The Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB)-Cagayan Valley said 29 barangays in the Batanes towns of

stories.title: PH braces for severe El Niño seen to last until 2027
stories.summary:
The Marcos administration is intensifying preparations to safeguard the Philippines' food, water, and energy supplies against a potentially severe El Niño phenomenon. Forecasts indicate that this climatic event could persist until 2027, posing significant threats to agricultural production and broader economic activity across the nation.

President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced on Monday that various government agencies are ramping up their mitigation efforts in response to these prolonged dry conditions. The administration aims to minimize disruptions to essential services and livelihoods as meteorological data points to an extended period of reduced rainfall.

links.headline: PH braces for severe El Niño seen to last until 2027
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — The Marcos administration is ramping up measures to protect the country’s food, water, and energy supplies amid warnings that a potentially severe El Niño phenomenon could persist until 2027, threatening agricultural production and economic activity nationwide. stories.title: Oriental Mindoro’s most wanted nabbed in manhunt operation
stories.summary:
Authorities in Oriental Mindoro have arrested the province’s most wanted individual during a joint manhunt operation. The suspect, identified as a 32-year-old fast-food restaurant supervisor from Puerto Galera, was apprehended on Monday in Barangay Zone 1 of Pinamalayan town.

The arrest was carried out by operatives from the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) Oriental Mindoro Provincial Field Unit. This operation successfully targeted the top provincial most wanted person, marking a significant law enforcement achievement for the region.

stories.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
stories.summary:
Italy has officially expressed support for Saudi Arabia to join the Global Combat Air Programme, according to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. This development marks a significant shift in the diplomatic and defense landscape surrounding the multinational fighter-jet initiative.

The program is currently led by the United Kingdom, Italy, and Japan, with the goal of developing a new stealth fighter capable of supersonic speeds by 2035. Saudi Arabia's potential inclusion would expand the consortium's industrial base and financial resources for this ambitious aerospace project.

links.headline: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
links.excerpt: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.
links.imageAlt: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
stories.summary:
Amazon is expanding its new conversational AI assistant, Alexa+, to India with a focus on local language support. The company has begun testing a Hindi-language version of the service within the country.

This move signals Amazon's intent to increase its footprint in the Indian market by making its advanced AI capabilities more accessible to non-English speaking users. The tech giant is currently inviting users in India to participate in testing phases for this localized iteration.

links.headline: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
links.excerpt: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.

stories.title: AI models capable of devastating attacks on governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns
stories.summary:
Intelligence agencies from the Five Eyes alliance have issued a rare joint warning that powerful AI models capable of devastating cyber attacks on governments and businesses are only months away. The statement, released by signals intelligence bodies in Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand, and Canada, urges global leaders to take immediate action to mitigate these emerging threats.

This public intervention follows recent developments involving Anthropic’s Fable AI model, which prompted former President Trump to block foreign nationals from accessing the technology. The coordinated message highlights growing concerns among allied nations regarding the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence capabilities and their potential misuse for large-scale cyber operations.

links.headline: AI models capable of devastating attacks on governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns

The surprising public intervention by signals agencies for Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada comes

stories.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
stories.summary:
Eight years after a historic peace accord dismantled Colombia’s most powerful rebel group, the country is facing renewed violence driven by cocaine-funded militias. These armed factions are increasingly targeting civilians, highlighting significant gaps in state security and governance.

The resurgence of these gangs underscores the challenges of maintaining stability following the withdrawal of major insurgent forces. Their operations are largely financed by the illicit drug trade, allowing them to operate with impunity in many regions.

links.headline: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
links.excerpt: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.
links.imageAlt: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
stories.summary:
Canada is emerging as a growing source of fentanyl production and trafficking, according to reports from the Wall Street Journal. While the volume remains significantly lower than that originating from Mexico, the shift highlights an evolving dynamic in North American drug enforcement.

This development places Canada under increased scrutiny, particularly as it intersects with broader U.S. political discourse. The rise in Canadian-manufactured fentanyl has drawn attention from policymakers and law enforcement agencies on both sides of the border.

The trend underscores the challenges faced by authorities in tracking illicit drug flows across international boundaries. As production networks adapt, the focus on Canada reflects a broader effort to address the root sources of the opioid crisis affecting the United States.

links.headline: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
links.excerpt: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.
links.imageAlt: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
